events.title: US Strikes Iran, Iran Strikes Gulf Neighbors, Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Following a series of U.S. strikes on Iranian targets, Iran retaliated by launching missiles and drones at U.S. facilities in multiple Gulf states, including Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman, and Jordan. Iran also announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil trade, further escalating regional tensions.

events.title: US CENTCOM Completes Third Round of Strikes on Iran
events.desc: The U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) announced the completion of its third round of strikes against Iranian targets, underscoring the ongoing military exchanges between the two nations. These strikes are in response to Iran's alleged attacks on commercial shipping.

events.title: IRGC Vows Crushing Response to Further US Attacks
events.desc: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a strong warning, vowing a 'crushing response' to any additional U.S. military actions against Iran. This statement indicates a readiness for further escalation.

events.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz After US Bombing
events.desc: In retaliation for U.S. bombing raids, Iran announced it had closed the Strait of Hormuz. This move, following previous hostilities and an MoU, severely impacts global shipping and increases regional instability.

events.title: Missiles and Drones Fired at Gulf States After US Strikes on Iran
events.desc: Following U.S. strikes on Iran, missiles and drones were launched at several Gulf nations, including the UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman, and Jordan. Three people were injured in Qatar due to falling shrapnel, indicating significant regional impact.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes; Tehran Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. and Iran engaged in significant missile and drone exchanges, with Iran targeting U.S. facilities across the Gulf and declaring the Strait of Hormuz closed. This escalation marks the end of a fragile ceasefire, although negotiations remain a possibility.

events.title: Iran's IRGC Navy Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Iran's Revolutionary Guards navy announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz until further notice, citing an incident involving a vessel attempting to transit an unapproved route. This action further tightens control over the vital waterway.

events.title: US Strikes Iran; Tehran Hits Gulf Neighbors
events.desc: Following new U.S. strikes, Iran announced it had closed the Strait of Hormuz and launched missiles and drones at its Gulf neighbors. These actions are in retaliation for a prior Iranian attack on a commercial vessel.

events.title: US Launches Fresh Strikes as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. military has conducted fresh strikes targeting Iran, prompting Iran to close the Strait of Hormuz. These actions follow an earlier attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel in the crucial waterway.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Strikes After Tankers Hit in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. military reported hitting missile sites and command centers in Iran, while Iran claimed to have attacked U.S. bases. This exchange of fire occurred after commercial tankers were targeted in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Trump Threatens More Strikes on Iran, Iran Warns of 'Fearless' Response
events.desc: President Trump indicated the U.S. would likely conduct further strikes on Iran, stating any future actions would be swift. Iran, in response, warned of a 'fearless' retaliation, heightening tensions between the two nations.

events.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Pledges Revenge for Slain Father and Predecessor
events.desc: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued a statement promising vengeance for the deaths of his father and predecessor. He stated that this revenge would depend not only on Iran but also on 'free people around the world'.
